creator: mva@FreeBSD.org
text:
(In reply to yuri from comment #0)
> This report to net@ from May 24:
> 
> > Status      |    Bug Id | Description
> > ------------+-----------+---------------------------------------------------
> > New         |    197535 | [re] [panic] if_re (Realtek 8168) causes memory w 
> > Open        |    194515 | Fatal Trap 12 Kernel with vimage                  
> > Open        |    199136 | [if_tap] Added down_on_close sysctl variable to t 
> >
> > 3 problems total for which you should take action.
> 
> includes https://bugs.freebsd.org/bugzilla/show_bug.cgi?id=199136, but
> doesn't include https://bugs.freebsd.org/bugzilla/show_bug.cgi?id=200185
> which is also assigned to net@, and also has Open status.

Correct and intended.
 
> There is something wrong with the query that retrieves the list, because
> both bug reports should be in the list.

No. The weekly reminder set up for FreeBSD's bugzilla system is not a query over all open bugs assigned to someone. Bugzilla's whine system fulfills that.
The weekly reminder only lists bugs, for which certain keywords or flags are set, reminding the assignee or requestee to react on it.
only.
